works.title: Minimalist Posters
works.description:
A stab at a corporate-minimalist style.

The QR-code-style glyphs come from the language I've designed for my Trepidation project. These simple posters are designs I can easily paste into established scenes to give the feel I'm looking for.

works.title: Trepidation Rooms
works.description: Some area concepts for my personal Trepidation project. These are created with a large focus on brutalist shape language.

works.title: Cover 11
works.description:
Another honing of graphic design.

The model was downloaded from Mixamo, but I remade all the materials to appear more photorealistic.

2nd image was a work in progress export.

works.title: Grungy Shower
works.description: An attempt at photorealism. Every material in this scene is procedural, even the shower head and drain.

works.title: Cover 09
works.description:
I wanted to make a purple-dominant design. This stock photo did the trick.

"PROPHECY" text made in blender.

works.title: Cover 06
works.description: Found a stock photo I liked and honed some ideas I had.

works.title: Cover 03
works.description:
Browsed for some stock photos to dress up.

This design is one of only a few that does not include additional information like the date or signature.

works.title: Translation
works.description:
Executing a concept for my Trepidation project. Working out how to handle the text was fun, the project overall was not too difficult.

I later rewrote this scene, so this animation is not too accurate anymore. I may remake it.

works.title: Cover 05
works.description: Another graphic design exploration. The graffiti was quite fun to make.

works.title: Cover 04
works.description: One of my fortes into graphic design. The ribcage and tendrils were hastily rendered in blender, all text and icons were added in post.

works.title: Gummy Art
works.description:
Took a stab at something like an album cover (an album called 'gummy', apparently).

It was fun to make but it doesn't look as delicious as it could be.

works.title: Colleague Face
works.description:
A render of a scene from my Trepidation project. Sculpted manually with 99% procedural materials (the LED panel wall in the back uses an image texture. That's it).

This piece involved use of almost every tab within the blender suite, which seriously tested my familarities and capability within the software. Although procedural, I still had to work with UVs on the character's skin and undershirt. The seam edges of the undershirt were done with geometry nodes, as well. 

2nd image excludes the graphics.

3rd image was the initial render, for the sake of framing.

4th video was a lackluster animation I whipped up a while later. Because the character was sculpted, I couldn't animate them at all; I had no further expectations and moved on to new projects.

works.title: Skull 01
works.description:
A Stylized design possibly fit for an album cover.

I imagined the skull's eyes as portals the gun was half inside. It might be interpreted differently. I think it looks cool either way.

works.title: Winged Scientist
works.description:
A non-canon depiction of a character from my Trepidation project.

This artwork originally existed only as linework and messy colors from two years prior (2nd image). I felt like drawing but didn't want to think of a pose so I dug this up, dusted it off, and cleaned it up.

The cityscape in the background was made in blender with a shader to simulate black outlines. After rendering, I did a lot of work to fix the colors and materials to have it fit in with the rest of the scene. The rings around the planet were also rendered in 3D, but as a separate layer from the cityscape. Everything else was done in 2D.

The entire artwork, from start to finish, was inspired by 'Reaper Hank' by Rhunyc on Newgrounds (3rd image).

works.title: Creeping Shade
works.description:
Character art for a card in my TCG Concept, Exul Omnium.

This card's abilities give it an 80% chance to deal overkill damage to nearby enemies.

It's working name was "Shade Watcher".

works.title: Dimension Jumper
works.description:
Character art for a card in my TCG Concept, Exul Omnium.

This card's abilities allow it to freely travel between your controlled lands, and upon death it has a 1/3 chance to respawn in any of those lands.

works.title: Procedural Land
works.description:
A messy attempt at a vast expanse. The ground is a single material with a thouroughly subdivided plane. The clouds are also procedural.

The 2nd image was my first render. Had to play around with a lot of the values to get something I was happy with, It also lacks any post-processing.

I forsee a remake of this shader material, something far more elaborate which could account for multiple biome types.

works.title: Skull 02
works.description: A stylized design possibly fit for an album cover.

works.title: Pyroclastic Elemental
works.description:
Character art for a card in my TCG Concept, Exul Omnium.

This card's abilities dictate the card can only stay alive for two turns, but every fire type it defeats extends that timer by one turn. It has a 50% to deal bonus damage to surrounding enemies, and it also explodes on death.

It's working name was "Fire Elemental".

works.title: Pixel Combat Sprites
works.description:
An extensive stylization of mine. Insipred from the works of Mostaza (6th image). 

This collection of sci-fi guns was a direct response to the initial collection I made two years prior (2nd image). I was not satisfied with a handful of designs from the original collection, but I would rather make new designs over remaking the old ones.

I've made a lot of sprites with this stylization.

works.title: Spiderman 2099
works.description:
Made a week before the release of Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse, because I liked the concept of the character. I knew nothing about him expect that he was probably the villian. After coming out of the theatre seeing him in action, I noticed about a dozen inaccuracies with my depiction of him. But, I never brought myself to alter the final piece.

The hexagons in the background were made in blender, the lots of filters and effects and edits were made to match it with the subject. I think it help sell the movie's aesthetic of 2D and 3D styles clashing.
